summary: Tested all 4 requested features: reorder-exercises endpoint (PASS), gender onboarding (PASS, Male/Female only, no Other), Cycle Cross Trainer GIF 2331 (PASS), drag handle rendering (PARTIAL - workout-detail had a critical missing ScrollView import that was fixed; but NestableDraggableFlatList crashes on web due to findNodeHandle not supported on web). Backend: 10/10 tests pass. Frontend: workout data loads correctly but exercise list crashes on web preview due to react-native-draggable-flatlist web incompatibility.

ui_bugs.issue: ScrollView was missing from React Native imports — caused 'ScrollView is not defined' crash. FIXED by adding ScrollView to import list.

findNodeHandle is not supported on web. Use the ref property on the component instead. — Crashes exercise list on web preview. This is a library web-incompatibility. On native (iOS/Android) this should work correctly.
ui_bugs.priority: MEDIUM (web-only crash; native should be unaffected)

react_native_issues.issue: Library calls findNodeHandle internally which is not supported on React Native Web. Stack trace: findNodeHandle -> useStableCallback -> identityRetainingCb -> onContainerLayout. This is a known web limitation of react-native-draggable-flatlist.

action_items: react-native-draggable-flatlist uses findNodeHandle which is not supported on web. For web preview compatibility, consider adding a web-specific fallback (e.g., render a regular FlatList on web via Platform.select). Native should be fine as-is.
Verify drag-to-reorder works on native device/emulator since web preview cannot test it due to findNodeHandle incompatibility.
critical_code_review_comments: workout-detail.tsx: ScrollView was used in JSX (line 656 — day tabs horizontal scroller) but was not imported from react-native. This caused a hard crash of the entire workout-detail screen. FIXED.
workout-detail.tsx: NestableDraggableFlatList from react-native-draggable-flatlist is not web-compatible due to findNodeHandle usage. This only impacts web preview; native builds should work.

success_rate.frontend: 60% — onboarding gender step PASS, workout data load PASS, drag handle rendering FAIL on web due to library incompatibility, ScrollView import bug FIXED

seed_data_creation: None. Used existing workout ID: dda90b0e-fcb2-45a8-9080-ef291af2500c
context_for_next_testing_agent: Workout ID dda90b0e-fcb2-45a8-9080-ef291af2500c has 6 exercises on day 0 (not 5 as noted in context). ScrollView import bug fixed. NestableDraggableFlatList web crash is a known library issue — test drag handle on native, not web preview. Backend reorder endpoint fully verified: 200 on valid payload, 404 on bad ID, 400 on length mismatch, 400 on bad day index. Gender onboarding: Male and Female only (Other removed) confirmed.
rca_of_issue: Root cause of workout-detail crash: ScrollView component was used in the JSX at line 656 (horizontal day tabs scroller) but was never imported from 'react-native'. The other imports (View, Text, etc.) were correct but ScrollView was missed when the day-tabs scroll view was added. Fix: add ScrollView to the react-native import block. Secondary issue: react-native-draggable-flatlist's NestableDraggableFlatList internally calls findNodeHandle (deprecated/unsupported on web in React 19/RN web). This only affects web preview — native builds are unaffected.
